title: Bridge Essay: Oxytocin's Regulation of Thermogenesis May Be the Link to Prader-Willi Syndrome.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Heat/Fire** (Phase 3) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Heat/Fire to mechanistic neuroscience through Oxytocin's, Regulation, of, Thermogenesis, May.

**Oxytocin's Regulation of Thermogenesis May Be the Link to Prader-Willi Syndrome.** — Curr Issues Mol Biol. PMID: 37367062. DOI: 10.3390/cimb45060313. Prader-Willi Syndrome (PWS) is agenetic neurodevelopmental disorder that is caused by either the deletion of the paternal allele of 15q11-q13, maternal uniparental disomy of chromosome 15 or defects in the chromosome 15 imprinting centre and is characterized by cognitive impairment, hyperphagia and low metabolic rate with significant risk of obesity, as well as avariety of other maladaptive behaviours and autistic spectrum disorder (ASD).
